page: Amazon Web Services g7e.4xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8559C processor providing 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores at a clock speed of 2.4 GHz. The CPU is equipped with a 320 MB L3 cache and supports advanced vector and matrix extensions, including AVX-512 and AMX. The instance is configured with 128.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 6 GHz, translating to 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

For accelerated workloads, the g7e.4xlarge includes a single NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is provided by a 1900 GB NVMe SSD, and the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 50 Gbps. This combination of high-capacity GPU memory, fast local storage, and high-speed networking supports data-intensive operations without external storage bottlenecks.

Performance benchmarks indicate top-tier capabilities in specific domains. The instance achieves top-tier results in Geekbench single-core and text processing tests. It also demonstrates top-tier performance in large language model inference, processing prompts and generating text efficiently across small, medium, and large models. Single-threaded compression and decompression tasks, such as gzip and bzip3, register in the top 25 percent, while multi-core CPU, memory bandwidth, and database operations perform in the average range.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the g7e.4xlarge offers high resource density by bundling a Blackwell-generation GPU, substantial DDR5 memory, and local NVMe storage on a single instance. This configuration minimizes the need for separate storage or memory provisioning, making it highly suitable for workloads such as LLM inference, graphics-intensive rendering, data science, and database operations requiring high-speed local caching.
description: Amazon Web Services g7e.4xlarge is a graphics-intensive server instance built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs from an Intel Xeon 8559C processor, 128.0 GB of 6 GHz DDR5 memory, and a 1900 GB local NVMe SSD. The hardware configuration is highlighted by a single NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB of VRAM and a baseline network speed of 50 Gbps. Benchmark data shows top-tier performance in single-core processing, text processing, and LLM inference across various model sizes, alongside strong single-threaded compression speeds. This resource-dense profile provides qualitative cost efficiency by bundling high-capacity GPU memory and fast local storage. The g7e.4xlarge is highly suited for LLM inference, graphics rendering, and data-intensive workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.4xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance featuring 16 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, a 1900 GB NVMe SSD, and an NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB VRAM.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g7e.4xlarge is a GPU-accelerated server with 16 vCPUs, 128 GB DDR5 RAM, and an NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU.
tagline: A high-density GPU instance featuring Blackwell architecture, fast NVMe storage, and top-tier large language model inference performance.
bullet_points: Equipped with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs and 128 GB of DDR5 memory.
Features a single NVIDIA Blackwell RTX Pro 6000 GPU with 96 GB VRAM.
Includes 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth.
Delivers top-tier benchmark performance for large language model inference.
Optimized for graphics-intensive rendering, data science, and database workloads.
